journal_entry.reason: Ford acquires land for factory construction with deferred payment terms. Under IAS 16.23, when payment is deferred beyond normal credit terms, the cost is the cash price equivalent (present value). The purchase price of $60,000,000 is to be paid in 6 annual instalments of $10,000,000 at 6% market rate. PV = sum of [10,000,000 / (1.06)^i for i in 1 to 6]. Calculation: Year 1: 9,433,962; Year 2: 8,899,964; Year 3: 8,396,192; Year 4: 7,920,936; Year 5: 7,472,581; Year 6: 7,049,605. Total PV = 49,173,240. The existing building will be demolished immediately; under IAS 16.16(b) and 16.20, costs of site preparation (including demolition) are capitalized into Land. Since the entire purchase was for land (building has no value given immediate demolition), the full PV is allocated to Land. The difference between face value ($60,000,000) and PV ($49,173,240) represents implicit interest of $10,826,760 to be recognized over the payment period.

output_debit_classifier.reason: Ford acquires land with an existing building for $60,000,000 to construct a new factory. The existing building will be demolished immediately. Under IAS 16.16(b), costs to prepare the site for its intended use (including demolition of existing structures) are capitalized into the Land account. The purchase price is payable in 6 equal annual instalments of $10,000,000 starting at the end of the acquisition year. Under IAS 16.23, when payment is deferred beyond normal credit terms, the asset is measured at the cash price equivalent (present value of payments). The market interest rate is 6%. Present value calculation: PV = 10,000,000/1.06 + 10,000,000/1.06² + 10,000,000/1.06³ + 10,000,000/1.06⁴ + 10,000,000/1.06⁵ + 10,000,000/1.06⁶ = 9,433,962 + 8,899,964 + 8,396,192 + 7,920,936 + 7,472,581 + 7,049,605 = 49,173,240. Land is recorded at this present value, and a corresponding liability is recognized. The difference between the face value ($60,000,000) and present value ($49,173,240) of $10,826,760 represents implicit interest to be recognized over the payment period using the effective interest method.
